other_observers: Norm Erthal, Steve Larson, Rachel Hopper
description: Bird found by Glenn Walbek.  First observed working a 4 ft post, in flight and then on tree trunks foraging. Woodpecker similar in structure to Red-bellied Woodpecker but slightly larger.  Female. Head shape was flatter and elongated from back of head to bill.  All white head with large dark eye.  A patch of yellow at the base of the bill. All white from throat, belly, flanks down to the undertail coverts which were slightly speckeled. Nape was yellow.  Black back with white barring.  All white rump and black tail feathers.  In flight, the outer primaries had three or so larger white spots. It had a long thick black bill.
